title: CI: integration timing results are randomly incomplete

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Yes but I seem to be the only one who cares

I just hit performance variations in the remote system tests in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8747.

@edsantiago and I looked into a VM together and noticed performance variations on Ubuntu 20.04.  https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8747 bumps the time out from 60 to 120 seconds.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@edsantiago @vrothberg Since we merged the bump can we close this issue?

The issue is not fixed. Example: [ubuntu 2010 log for PR 9082](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6211303586922496/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2010-root-host.log.html). Search in-page for "preserves container". The test is run, but there is no summary line. Compare to [ubuntu 2004 log for same PR](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4803928703369216/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2004-root-host.log.html). Here the test is run and also summarized at bottom.

This is a problem because we still don't understand why ubuntu 2010 runs slower than 2004, and without logged timing results there is no possible way to actually look into the difference.


I really feel like this must be a trivial one-line fix for someone who understands Go arrays and threads.
